page: Amazon Web Services m5a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs, derived from 48 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This server is built on the Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation, but does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance tradeoffs. Single-core CPU performance is limited, scoring in the bottom 10% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. However, the instance scales effectively in multi-threaded environments, achieving average performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, PassMark tests, and software compilation. Memory performance is a notable strength, with both cached and uncached read/write memory bandwidth ranking in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations and Redis SET operations also demonstrate strong performance, reaching the top 25% tier.

Given its hardware profile, the m5a.24xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and parallel processing rather than raw single-core speed. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded database operations, caching layers such as Redis, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications that require a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio.
description: Amazon Web Services m5a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides dedicated CPU allocation at 2.5 GHz but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. While its single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10%, its multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are highly competitive, with memory read/write speeds and encryption benchmarks reaching the top 25%. With a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps, this instance is well-suited for multi-threaded database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC 7571 processors, offering strong memory bandwidth and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, delivering strong memory bandwidth for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity, general-purpose platform featuring balanced memory bandwidth and multi-core scalability for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors
Strong memory bandwidth performance ranking in the top 25% for cached and uncached operations
Average multi-core CPU scaling despite lower single-core benchmark results
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with dedicated CPU allocation on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for multi-threaded database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose applications
